compiled_state.author: Michael Giver

comments.author: Michael Giver
comments.body: Naming-drift risk for the id->rule mapping (surfaced in #751 review): the config id is 'american_indian_alaska_native' (no 'or') while Rules::ExclusionRuleset uses 'american_indian_or_alaska_native' (with 'or', in AMERICAN_INDIAN_OR_ALASKA_NATIVE + the race_ethnicity value). Different namespaces today so not a current bug, but whoever wires id->rule here must not let the off-by-one-word silently miss.

comments.author: Michael Giver
comments.body:
Scope confirmed (Michael, 2026-07-08): proceed on the CURRENT rules-engine architecture. (a) Collapse multi-match to the single highest-priority exemption instead of returning an unsorted list; (b) drop age (under-19/over-65) from the exclusion path per Jeff â€” state won't send out-of-age-range members (see also CMS Â§435.553: age is a mandatory exception, not exclusion); (c) remove now-dead DOB/evaluated_on extraction.

FORWARD NOTES for the future ARIES API adapter (out of scope here):
1. Name reconciliation â€” the ARIES contract (Data Dictionary spec, doc 1cBEzâ€¦) sends a flat `member_data.exclusions_and_exceptions[]` array of `{name, value, verification_status}` with a 4th naming vocabulary that diverges from our config ids: `american_indian_or_alaska_native` (the "or" returns), `pregnant_or_postpartum`, `medical_condition` (â‰ˆ medically_frail), `caregiver_child`/`caregiver_disability`, `complies_with_atap/snap_work_requirements`, `substance_treatment`, `inmate`+`recently_released` (â‰ˆ our `incarceration`). Adapter must map explicitly â€” do NOT string-munge (extends the idâ†”fact drift risk already noted below).
2. `verification_status` (verified/unverified) becomes a factor the selection logic must weigh â€” not modeled today.
3. Compute-vs-receive + dates is UNRESOLVED in the spec: the request shape sends pre-computed booleans (no dates â†’ OSCER just picks), but Jeff D (spec comment 2026-07-07) argues OSCER should make time-based judgments (exclusion true during lookback but not now â†’ "excepted"; 12-mo postpartum window), which needs per-exemption dates. If that lands, "stop-at-first == best" must be revisited (the PM dynamic-durability flag) and dates reintroduced in a new shape.

The return-best SELECTION logic built here is durable across the array-model rewrite; only the input plumbing gets replaced.

comments.author: Michael Giver
comments.body:
Implemented 2026-07-08 (branch giverm/OSCER-752-exclusion-determination-return-best; not yet committed/pushed).

4-file change:
- Rules::ExclusionRuleset â€” dropped age (`age_under_19` method + the two age params from `eligible_for_exclusion`); added explicit `EXCLUSION_FACT_IDS` bridge (fact name -> config id, no string-munging).
- ExclusionDeterminationService â€” removed dead `extract_date_of_birth`/`extract_evaluation_date` + their facts; `highest_priority_reason_code` selects via `min_by { exclusion_priority(fact.name) }` (fail-loud `.fetch` on the bridge + `Exclusion.find`); passes `[reason_code]` to recording.
- CertificationCase#record_exclusion_determination â€” now takes `reason_codes` (mirrors the existing `record_exception_determination` sibling); service owns selection.
- Determination â€” deleted `to_reason_codes` (it was the old "return unsorted list of all" behavior; no direct spec).

Collateral specs updated: exclusion_ruleset_spec (dropped `#age_under_19`, `eligible_for_exclusion` -> 3-arg), certification_case_spec + certification_business_process_spec (new reason_codes signature, removed dead fact lets).

Verification: full suite 2549 ex 0 failures, 96.45% line / 83.46% branch cov, RuboCop clean. Net -235 lines. Spec contract: recorded determination `reasons` = single-element highest-priority array (incl. a config-override test proving priority is config-driven, not hardcoded). NEXT: /review then PR.

comments.author: Michael Giver
comments.body:
/review (3-agent) PASSED â€” min score 4/5.
- Security (Opus): vulnerability 5, input_validation 5, authz/output N/A â€” background service, no user-facing surface, no findings.
- Context (Haiku): 5/5/5 â€” all 3 ACs met (single highest-priority recorded; flow unchanged; priority-selection + stop-at-first + config-override tests present); no scope creep; aligned with Data Source Hierarchy direction.
- Quality (Sonnet): maintainability 4, test_coverage 4, rails_conventions 5, reliability 4.

Applied the Quality should-fixes (all PASS-level polish, no behavior change):
1. Fixed stale YARD `@param eligibility_fact` doc on record_exclusion_determination (now reason_codes).
2. exclusion_priority now genuinely fails loud: `Exclusion.find(id) || raise(KeyError, "...id ... bridged from fact ...")` instead of a bare NoMethodError on nil; comment corrected.
3. Renamed spec contexts exemption -> exclusion (this service is exclusively exclusions).
4. Added guard specs: EXCLUSION_FACT_IDS invariants (every bridged id is configured w/ priority; every key maps to a reason code) + a service test exercising the fail-loud raise.

Re-verified: full suite 2552 ex 0 failures, 96.45/83.46 cov, RuboCop clean. Ready for PR.

comments.author: Michael Giver
comments.body:
Shipped: committed a3952f4f, pushed, PR #761 open against main (https://github.com/navapbc/oscer/pull/761).

CORRECTION to the earlier forward note (#3): the flat `exclusions_and_exceptions` verified-boolean array was RedMane's initial proposal and is NOT the direction. It can't support the rules OSCER must implement â€” per Jeff (2026-07-07), an exclusion active only during the lookback becomes a mandatory exception (plus 12-mo postpartum windows), so OSCER makes the time-based judgment and needs per-exemption date context, not flat booleans. Compute-vs-receive resolves toward OSCER computes. So for the future adapter: expect richer/dated exemption input, not a flat boolean array; the return-best selection built here is the durable core the exception band + data-source ordering extend. (Naming-reconciliation + verification_status notes from #1/#2 still stand.)

compiled_state.description: GH #752. Fast-follow to #751. Update ExclusionDeterminationService to iterate enabled exclusions in Exclusion.priority_order, stop at first true (== best while priority is static), record/return that one. Out of scope: Data Source Registry. RISK (PM 2026-07-07): stop-at-first == best only while durability is a static list; revisit when durability goes dynamic.
compiled_state.title: ExclusionDeterminationService: return highest-priority exclusion instead of unsorted list
